Now consider this scenario again – could we use the same logic to derive total live births from total deaths instead?

Let’s explore this next step logically.

What would happen if someone claimed we could calculate total deaths from a death rate?

Would the same flaws apply? Yes—and more so because mortality often involves non-live outcomes (e.g., accidents), absence of official reporting during certain periods (like epidemics), and high variability among regions and ages.

Therefore, analogies help expose blind spots in epidemiological estimates—even when models appear simple at first glance.

Well said!
